title: Publications
text:
Mes Publications

(Jacquet et al. 2020; C. Baumas et al. 2023, 2024; Fuchs et al. 2022; Tanet et al. 2019; Garel et al. 2019; Benavides et al. 2022; Pradel et al. 2013, 2013; Tamburini, Goutx, et al. 2009, 2009; Zhang et al. 2016; Amrani et al. 2014; Severin et al. 2016; La Rivière, Garel, and Bally 2016; Riou et al. 2018; Michotey et al. 2020, 2020; Van Wambeke et al. 2020; acquet_particulate_2020?; Roumagnac et al. 2020; C. M. J. Baumas et al. 2021; Quéméneur et al. 2019; Khelaifia et al. 2011; Smedile et al. 2015; Al Ali et al. 2010; Boutrif et al. 2011; Cathalot et al. 2021; Tamburini et al. 2021; garel_ready?–use_2020; S. Martini et al. 2016; Séverine Martini et al. 2013; Tamburini, Garel, et al. 2009; Tamburini et al. 2013; Garel et al. 2021; Fauvelle et al. 2021; Fenouil et al. 2023; Schmidt et al. 2025; C. Baumas et al. 2025; Le Coq et al. 2026)



Al Ali, Badr, Marc Garel, Philippe Cuny, Juan-Carlos Miquel, Tarik Toubal, Anne Robert, and Christian Tamburini. 2010. “Luminous Bacteria in the Deep-Sea Waters Near the ANTARES Underwater Neutrino Telescope (Mediterranean Sea).” Chemistry and Ecology 26 (1): 57–72. https://doi.org/10.1080/02757540903513766.


Amrani, Amira, Aurélie Bergon, Hélène Holota, Christian Tamburini, Marc Garel, Bernard Ollivier, Jean Imbert, Alain Dolla, and Nathalie Pradel. 2014. “Transcriptomics Reveal Several Gene Expression Patterns in the Piezophile Desulfovibrio Hydrothermalis in Response to Hydrostatic Pressure.” PLoS ONE 9 (9). https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0106831.


Baumas, Chloé M. J., Frédéric A. C. Le Moigne, Marc Garel, Nagib Bhairy, Sophie Guasco, Virginie Riou, Fabrice Armougom, Hans-Peter Grossart, and Christian Tamburini. 2021. “Mesopelagic Microbial Carbon Production Correlates with Diversity Across Different Marine Particle Fractions.” The ISME Journal, January. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41396-020-00880-z.


Baumas, Chloé, Fatima-Ezzahra Ababou, Marc Garel, Mina Bizic, Danny Ionescu, Arthur Puzenat, Frederic Le Moigne, Hans-Peter Grossart, and Christian Tamburini. 2024. “A Novel Method to Sample Individual Marine Snow Particles for Downstream Molecular Analyses.” Limnology and Oceanography Methods, January. https://doi.org/10.1002/lom3.10590.


Baumas, Chloé, Robin Fuchs, Marc Garel, Jean-Christophe Poggiale, Laurent Memery, Frederic Le Moigne, and Christian Tamburini. 2023. “Reconstructing the Ocean’s Mesopelagic Zone Carbon Budget: Sensitivity and Estimation of Parameters Associated with Prokaryotic Remineralization.” Journal of Geophysical Research Atmospheres 20 (October): 4165–82. https://doi.org/10.5194/bg-20-4165-2023.


Baumas, Chloé, Danny Ionescu, Marc Garel, Hans-Peter Grossart, Christian Tamburini, and Mina Bizic. 2025. Pressure-Driven Microbial and Viral Dynamics on Individual Sinking Particles: Implications for Carbon Cycling. https://doi.org/10.31223/X5FJ2Q.


Benavides, Mar, Sophie Bonnet, Frédéric A. C. Le Moigne, Gabrielle Armin, Keisuke Inomura, Søren Hallstrøm, Lasse Riemann, et al. 2022. “Sinking Trichodesmium Fixes Nitrogen in the Dark Ocean.” The ISME Journal, July, 1–8. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41396-022-01289-6.


Boutrif, Mehdi, Marc Garel, Matthew T. Cottrell, and Christian Tamburini. 2011. “Assimilation of Marine Extracellular Polymeric Substances by Deep-Sea Prokaryotes in the NW Mediterranean Sea.” Environmental Microbiology Reports 3 (6): 705–9. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1758-2229.2011.00285.x.


Cathalot, Cécile, Erwan G. Roussel, Antoine Perhirin, Vanessa Creff, Jean-Pierre Donval, Vivien Guyader, Guillaume Roullet, et al. 2021. “Hydrothermal Plumes as Hotspots for Deep-Ocean Heterotrophic Microbial Biomass Production.” Nature Communications 12 (1): 6861. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-021-26877-6.


Fauvelle, Vincent, Marc Garel, Christian Tamburini, David Nerini, Javier Castro-Jiménez, Natascha Schmidt, Andrea Paluselli, et al. 2021. “Organic Additive Release from Plastic to Seawater Is Lower Under Deep-Sea Conditions.” Nature Communications 12 (1): 4426. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-021-24738-w.


Fenouil, Romain, Nathalie Pradel, Hassiba Belahbib, Marie Roumagnac, Manon Bartoli, Wajdi Ben Hania, Yann Denis, et al. 2023. “Adaptation Strategies to High Hydrostatic Pressures in Pseudothermotoga Species Revealed by Transcriptional Analyses.” Microorganisms 11 (3): 773. https://doi.org/10.3390/microorganisms11030773.


Fuchs, Robin, Chloé M. J. Baumas, Marc Garel, David Nerini, Frédéric A. C. Le Moigne, and Christian Tamburini. 2022. “A RUpture-Based Detection Method for the Active mesopeLagIc Zone (RUBALIZ): A Crucial Step Toward Rigorous Carbon Budget Assessments.” Limnology and Oceanography: Methods n/a. https://doi.org/10.1002/lom3.10520.


Garel, Marc, Patricia Bonin, Séverine Martini, Sophie Guasco, Marie Roumagnac, Nagib Bhairy, Fabrice Armougom, and Christian Tamburini. 2019. “Pressure-Retaining Sampler and High-Pressure Systems to Study Deep-Sea Microbes Under in Situ Conditions.” Frontiers in Microbiology 10. https://doi.org/10.3389/fmicb.2019.00453.


Garel, Marc, Christos Panagiotopoulos, Mehdi Boutrif, Daniel Repeta, Richard Sempéré, Chiara Santinelli, Bruno Charrière, David Nerini, Jean-Christophe Poggiale, and Christian Tamburini. 2021. “Contrasting Degradation Rates of Natural Dissolved Organic Carbon by Deep-Sea Prokaryotes Under Stratified Water Masses and Deep-Water Convection Conditions in the NW Mediterranean Sea.” Marine Chemistry 231 (April): 103932. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.marchem.2021.103932.


Jacquet, S H M, D Lefevre, Christian Tamburini, Marc Garel, Frédéric A. C. Le Moigne, Nagib Bhairy, Marie Roumagnac, and Sophie Guasco. 2020. “On the Barium - Oxygen Consumption Relationship in the Mediterranean Sea: Implications for Mesopelagic Marine Snow Remineralization.” Biogeosciences Discussion. https://doi.org/10.5194/bg-2020-241.


Khelaifia, Saber, Marie-Laure Fardeau, Nathalie Pradel, Clément Aussignargues, Marc Garel, Christian Tamburini, Jean-Luc Cayol, Sylvie Gaudron, Françoise Gaill, and Bernard Ollivier. 2011. “Desulfovibrio Piezophilus Sp. Nov., a Piezophilic, Sulfate-Reducing Bacterium Isolated from Wood Falls in the Mediterranean Sea.” International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology 61 (November): 2706–11. https://doi.org/10.1099/ijs.0.028670-0.


La Rivière, Marie, Marc Garel, and Marc Bally. 2016. “Localization of Endobacteria in the Gastrodermis of a Mediterranean Gorgonian Coral, Paramuricea Clavata, Using Fluorescence in Situ Hybridization.” Marine Biology 163 (10): 206. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00227-016-2975-y.


Le Coq, Pauline, Urania Christaki, France Van Wambeke, Elisabeth Chevillon, Bruno Zakardjian, Marc Garel, Sophie Guasco, et al. 2026. “Distinct Contributions of Suspended and Sinking Prokaryotes to Mesopelagic Carbon Budget.” Nature Geoscience, January, 1–8. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41561-025-01888-w.


Martini, Séverine, Badr Al Ali, Marc Garel, David Nerini, Vincent Grossi, Muriel Pacton, Laurence Casalot, Philippe Cuny, and Christian Tamburini. 2013. “Effects of Hydrostatic Pressure on Growth and Luminescence of a Moderately-Piezophilic Luminous Bacteria Photobacterium Phosphoreum ANT-2200.” Edited by Adam Driks. PLoS ONE 8 (6): e66580. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0066580.


Martini, S., V. Michotey, L. Casalot, P. Bonin, S. Guasco, M. Garel, and C. Tamburini. 2016. “Bacteria as Part of Bioluminescence Emission at the Deep ANTARES Station (North-Western Mediterranean Sea) During a One-Year Survey.” Deep Sea Research Part I: Oceanographic Research Papers 116: 33–40. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.dsr.2016.07.014.


Michotey, Valérie, Aurélie Blanfuné, Cristèle Chevalier, Marc Garel, Frédéric Diaz, Léo Berline, Louis Le Grand, et al. 2020. “In Situ Observations and Modelling Revealed Environmental Factors Favouring Occurrence of Vibrio in Microbiome of the Pelagic Sargassum Responsible for Strandings.” Science of the Total Environment 748 (December). https://doi.org/10.1016/j.scitotenv.2020.141216.


Pradel, Nathalie, Boyang Ji, Grégory Gimenez, Emmanuel Talla, Patricia Lenoble, Marc Garel, Christian Tamburini, et al. 2013. “The First Genomic and Proteomic Characterization of a Deep-Sea Sulfate Reducer: Insights into the Piezophilic Lifestyle of Desulfovibrio Piezophilus.” PloS One 8 (1): e55130. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0055130.


Quéméneur, Marianne, Gaël Erauso, Eléonore Frouin, Emna Zeghal, Céline Vandecasteele, Bernard Ollivier, Christian Tamburini, Marc Garel, Bénédicte Ménez, and Anne Postec. 2019. “Hydrostatic Pressure Helps to Cultivate an Original Anaerobic Bacterium from the Atlantis Massif Subseafloor (IODP Expedition 357): Petrocella Atlantisensis Gen. Nov. Sp. Nov.” Frontiers in Microbiology 10 (July): 1497. https://doi.org/10.3389/fmicb.2019.01497.


Riou, Virginie, Julien Para, Marc Garel, Catherine Guigue, Badr Al Ali, Chiara Santinelli, Dominique Lefèvre, et al. 2018. “Biodegradation of Emiliania Huxleyi Aggregates by a Natural Mediterranean Prokaryotic Community Under Increasing Hydrostatic Pressure.” Progress in Oceanography 163 (April): 271–81. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.pocean.2017.01.005.


Roumagnac, Marie, Nathalie Pradel, Manon Bartoli, Marc Garel, Aaron A. Jones, Fabrice Armougom, Romain Fenouil, et al. 2020. “Responses to the Hydrostatic Pressure of Surface and Subsurface Strains of Pseudothermotoga Elfii Revealing the Piezophilic Nature of the Strain Originating from an Oil-Producing Well.” Frontiers in Microbiology 11 (December): 588771. https://doi.org/10.3389/fmicb.2020.588771.


Schmidt, Natascha, Aurelio Foscari Widmann Rezzonico, Dorte Herzke, Marc Garel, Christian Tamburini, Bettina Seiwert, Thorsten Reemtsma, and Richard Sempéré. 2025. “Aging of Tire Particles in Deep-Sea Conditions: Interactions Between Hydrostatic Pressure, Prokaryotic Growth and Chemical Leaching.” Environmental Science & Technology 59 (September). https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.est.5c10705.


Severin, Tatiana, Caroline Sauret, Mehdi Boutrif, Thomas Duhaut, Fayçal Kessouri, Louise Oriol, Jocelyne Caparros, et al. 2016. “Impact of an Intense Water Column Mixing (0-1500 m) on Prokaryotic Diversity and Activities During an Open-Ocean Convection Event in the NW Mediterranean Sea.” Environmental Microbiology 18 (12): 4378–90. https://doi.org/10.1111/1462-2920.13324.


Smedile, Francesco, Simona Scarfi, Emilio De Domenico, Marc Garel, and Helen Glanville. 2015. “Variations in Microbial Community Structure Through the Stratified Water Column in the Tyrrhenian Sea ( Central Mediterranean ).” Journal of Marine Science and Engineering 3: 845–65. https://doi.org/10.3390/jmse3030845.


Tamburini, Christian, Mehdi Boutrif, Marc Garel, Rita R Colwell, and Jody W Deming. 2013. “Prokaryotic Responses to Hydrostatic Pressure in the Ocean - a Review.” Environmental Microbiology 15 (5): 1262–74. https://doi.org/10.1111/1462-2920.12084.


Tamburini, Christian, Marc Garel, Badr Al Ali, Bastien Mérigot, Pascal Kriwy, Bruno Charrière, and Giorgio Budillon. 2009. “Distribution and Activity of Bacteria and Archaea in the Different Water Masses of the Tyrrhenian Sea.” Deep Sea Research Part II: Topical Studies in Oceanography 56 (11): 700–712. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.dsr2.2008.07.021.


Tamburini, Christian, Marc Garel, Aude Barani, Dominique Boeuf, Patricia Bonin, Nagib Bhairy, Sophie Guasco, et al. 2021. “Increasing Hydrostatic Pressure Impacts the Prokaryotic Diversity During Emiliania Huxleyi Aggregates Degradation.” Water 13 (19): 2616. https://doi.org/10.3390/w13192616.


Tamburini, Christian, Madeleine Goutx, Catherine Guigue, Marc Garel, Dominique Lefèvre, Bruno Charrière, Richard Sempéré, et al. 2009. “Effects of Hydrostatic Pressure on Microbial Alteration of Sinking Fecal Pellets.” Deep Sea Research Part II: Topical Studies in Oceanography 56 (18): 1533–46. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.dsr2.2008.12.035.


Tanet, Lisa, Christian Tamburini, Chloé Baumas, Marc Garel, Gwénola Simon, and Laurie Casalot. 2019. “Bacterial Bioluminescence: Light Emission in Photobacterium Phosphoreum Is Not Under Quorum-Sensing Control.” Frontiers in Microbiology 10 (March). https://doi.org/10.3389/fmicb.2019.00365.


Van Wambeke, France, Elvira Pulido, Julie Dinasquet, Kahina Djaoudi, Anja Engel, Marc Garel, Sophie Guasco, et al. 2020. “Spatial Patterns of Biphasic Ectoenzymatic Kinetics Related to Biogeochemical Properties in the Mediterranean Sea.” Biogeosciences Discussions. https://doi.org/10.5194/bg-2020-253.

title: Projet 1 : Analyse 16S
text:
Contexte
Ce projet vise à automatiser l’analyse de données de séquençage 16S pour des échantillons marins sous haute pression.

title: Recherche
text:
Bioinformatique : Analyse de données 16S, métagénomique.
Instrumentation : Développement de systèmes de prélèvement sous haute pression.
Océanographie : Étude des écosystèmes marins profonds.




Projet HyperbaricTech : Développement de nouveaux compensateurs de pression.

title: Recherche
text: Projet HyperbaricTech : Développement de nouveaux compensateurs de pression.
